name: Japan Open
stakes: This Super 750 event is a critical test for India's top shuttlers to regain form, build momentum, and assert their presence on the global stage, especially for those returning from injury or seeking consistency.
summary:
India's badminton contingent arrives in Tokyo for the Japan Open, a Super 750 event, with a mix of anticipation and caution. All eyes will be on the men's doubles pair of Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty, who return as the third seeds after pulling out of the Indonesia Open due to Satwik's recurring shoulder injury. Their performance will be a key indicator of their readiness for the demanding season ahead.

In the singles categories, PV Sindhu will be looking to bounce back after an early exit in Indonesia, aiming to find the consistent form that defines her best play. Lakshya Sen, still reeling from a yellow card incident at the Singapore Open, will seek redemption and a deep run to demonstrate his focus. Meanwhile, Kidambi Srikanth and Rounak Chouhan, fresh off strong showings at the US Open—with Srikanth reaching the final and Chouhan upsetting a World No. 6—will be eager to carry that momentum into this prestigious tournament. The women's doubles pair of Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand, part of the Asian Games squad, also look to build on their progress.
